title: JavaScript Comments
content:
### Why Use Comments?

Comments help you and others **understand** your code. They are **ignored** by JavaScript and are only for humans.

---

### Comment Types

#### 1. Single-Line Comment

Use `//` for one line of explanation.

```js
// This is a single-line comment
let x = 5; // This also works at the end of a line
```

#### 2. Multi-Line Comment

Use `/* ... */` for longer explanations.

```js
/*
  This is a multi-line comment.
  You can write several lines here.
  Useful for describing complex logic.
*/
```

---

### When to Comment

| Use Case | Example |
|----------|---------|
| **Explain complex logic** | Why this algorithm works |
| **Document functions** | What params and return values are |
| **Temporarily disable code** | Add `//` before a line to skip it |
| **Todo reminders** | `// TODO: optimize this loop` |

---

> **Good practice:** Explain the **why**, not the **what**. Code itself shows what happens.

quiz.question: Which symbol is used for single-line comments in JavaScript?
